question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the getNftAllowances() endpoint with high-volume concurrent requests using crafted NftAllowanceRequest objects, exhausting database connection pool resources and causing the mirror node to fail to ingest or propagate gossip transactions, resulting in a sustained denial-of-service against transaction gossip processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on getNftAllowances() to issue thousands of requests per second with varying accountId values, causing sustained high database load that prevents the mirror node from keeping up with gossip transaction ingestion, effectively suppressing transaction propagation?
